title: Caramelised Parsnips with Honey, Sage & Hazelnut Dust
summary: These sticky, honey-roasted parsnips are a beautiful combination of sweet and savoury. Perfect as a side for Christmas, Thanksgiving or any big old feast.

method.description: Heat the oven to 190°C / 375°F. Peel the parsnips and cut them into long wedges. Finely chop the sage and crush the hazelnuts.
In a large ovenproof skillet, melt the butter over a medium heat. Add the parsnips and cook until golden. Drizzle with honey, add sage, and toss to coat.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and roast for 15-20 minutes, until the parsnips are tender and caramelised.
Sprinkle the caramelised parsnips with hazelnut dust and serve warm.

chefs.name: Laurent Dagenais
chefs.summary:
Born and raised in Montreal, Dagenais is a chef by training who worked in the restaurant industry for almost a decade before a snowboarding accident changed his trajectory. “A decade ago my career took a turn that I would have never predicted,” explained Dagenais. “Breaking my arm put my cooking life on hold and that's why, in the midst of the pandemic, I started making videos on the internet.”
 
It was these videos that changed his life, making him an instant social media phenomenon. Reaching over 4+ million followers across his channels, Dagenais' main goal is to share his love of food and inspire people to get in to the kitchen more often.
